trajectory.description: Observation: empty workspace. Analysis: need establish a working end-to-end PyG pipeline quickly per instructions, with a short 2-epoch baseline and immediate eval to validate predict.py and measure runtime. Decision: implement a compact OGB-style GINE graph model with atom/bond encoders, masked BCE loss with per-task positive weights, validation AP logging, checkpoint saving, and offline prediction script.

Observation: initial end-to-end run failed after ~23 minutes with std::bad_alloc before eval. Analysis: most likely CPU RAM/shared-memory pressure from PyG DataLoader workers holding a huge in-memory torch.load dictionary, possibly amplified by multiprocessing and large batches. Decision: make the pipeline memory-safe by forcing num_workers=0 by default, lowering batch sizes/model size for a quick baseline, adding checkpoint saving even if validation is costly, and retrying 2 epochs with immediate eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: both attempted GNN training runs failed before any eval, first with bad_alloc after a long run and second with a fast exit 139. Analysis: the highest priority is to validate the evaluation path; a constant per-task prevalence baseline is memory/lightweight, avoids PyG training/validation, and provides a checkpoint/predict.py contract sanity check. Decision: add support for a tiny prior-only checkpoint in predict.py, create it from labels in train.pt with no graph batching, and immediately submit eval.

print('\ntrain_more_v2 argparse/default lines:')

for i,l in enumerate(s):

        print(f'{i+1}: {l}')
print('\ncheckpoints:')
